subject: a11y(components): add missing aria-labels + swap data-testid for semantic query in test
body:
Three findings from the parallel a11y audit, all applied. Patterns and
simplify audits returned clean ("No actionable findings" and "branch is
as simple as it should be").

- ContractUploadPreview rename button now has aria-label="Rename
  section <title>" — screen readers previously heard only the module
  title, losing the cue that it's clickable to edit.
- ToneSelector Add button now has aria-label dynamically derived from
  the component's label prop: "Add custom tone" in ToneSelector's
  default usage, "Add custom intent" when IntentSelector wraps it. The
  bare "Add" accessible name was ambiguous to screen readers.
- Test query updated to match the new accessible name (getByRole
  {name: /add custom tone/i}).
- NotificationBell outside-click test: swapped the data-testid dummy
  for a semantic <button>outside</button> + getByRole('button', {name:
  'outside'}). Aligns the test with accessibility-first query
  discipline and matches the rest of the suite.

Auth-bypass banner noted by the audit as borderline (role="status" +
polite vs. the offline banner's explicit aria-live="assertive")
intentionally left alone — it's a dev-only visual reminder, not a
user-facing critical alert, so polite is the right tier.

Tests verified: 155/155 passing. tsc -b clean, eslint clean.

Tags: a11y, aria, tests
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: custom-code-reduction
Semver: patch
